title: TypeScript Strings
content:
### String Type

The `string` type represents textual data.

### String Declaration

```typescript
let single: string = 'Single quotes';
let double: string = "Double quotes";
let template: string = `Template literals`;
```

### Template Literals

Template literals support interpolation and multi-line strings:

```typescript
let name: string = "Alice";
let age: number = 30;
let greeting: string = `Hello, ${name}! You are ${age} years old.`;

let multiLine: string = `
  This is a
  multi-line string
`;
```

### Common String Methods

| Method | Description | Example |
|--------|------------|--------|
| `.length` | Returns string length | `"hello".length` |
| `.toUpperCase()` | Converts to uppercase | `"hello".toUpperCase()` |
| `.toLowerCase()` | Converts to lowercase | `"HELLO".toLowerCase()` |
| `.includes()` | Checks if substring exists | `"hello".includes("ell")` |
| `.startsWith()` | Checks prefix | `"hello".startsWith("he")` |
| `.endsWith()` | Checks suffix | `"hello".endsWith("lo")` |
| `.indexOf()` | Find index of substring | `"hello".indexOf("l")` |
| `.slice()` | Extract a portion | `"hello".slice(1, 4)` |
| `.replace()` | Replace substring | `"hello".replace("l", "x")` |
| `.split()` | Split into array | `"a,b,c".split(",")` |
| `.trim()` | Remove whitespace | `" hello ".trim()` |

### TypeScript Specifics

```typescript
// String literal types
let direction: "left" | "right" = "left";

// Template literal types
type EventName = `on${Capitalize<string>}`;
```

> **Key Point:** Strings are immutable in TypeScript/JavaScript.
